question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the error message in the thrown SignatureVerificationException — which leaks internal state including the filename, debugStake, and totalStake values — to perform reconnaissance on the network's stake distribution, enabling a more targeted attack to prevent gossip of specific high-value transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a collection of StreamFileSignature objects where all signatures have the same node identity but different fileHash values, causing each hash group to contain only one node's stake, and since a single node's stake is unlikely to exceed stakeRequiredForConsensus alone, preventing consensus from being reached and blocking gossip of all transactions in the targeted stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not validate that all signatures in the collection belong to the same stream file (filename is only read from the first signature), by injecting signatures from different stream files into the same collection, causing the consensus check to be performed against a mixed set of signatures that may never reach consensus for any single file, blocking gossip of all targeted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a StreamFileSignature with status VERIFIED but with a node whose getStake() returns Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the stake accumulation loop to overflow to Long.MIN_VALUE when combined with other stakes, making canReachConsensus() return false even when the legitimate stake threshold is met, and preventing gossip of all transactions in the targeted stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the consensusRatio == BigDecimal.ZERO check uses BigDecimal.ZERO.equals() (value equality) rather than compareTo(), meaning that a consensusRatio of 0.0 with a different scale (e.g., BigDecimal('0.00')) would not match, causing the bypass to fail and requiring full consensus even when the operator intended to disable it, potentially blocking gossip of transactions in edge-case configurations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a collection of StreamFileSignature objects where the first signature has a node with getTotalStake() equal to Long.MAX_VALUE, causing getStakeRequiredForConsensus() to compute a BigDecimal threshold that overflows or produces an astronomically large value, making canReachConsensus() always return false and permanently blocking gossip of all transactions processed by this validator instance?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a check for duplicate node IDs in the signatures collection, by injecting multiple StreamFileSignature objects all claiming to be from the same high-stake node with the same fileHash, causing the stake to be summed multiple times for the same node and artificially inflating the stake for a minority hash group to reach CONSENSUS_REACHED, allowing gossip of a fraudulent transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a StreamFileSignature with status VERIFIED and a fileHash that is an empty byte array (new byte[0]), causing getFileHashAsHex() to return an empty string that acts as a special key in signatureHashMap, potentially grouping with other empty-hash signatures and creating a phantom consensus group that causes the wrong transaction data to be gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not verify that the SignatureStatus transitions are valid (e.g., a signature going from NOT_FOUND directly to VERIFIED is not checked), by pre-setting injected signatures to VERIFIED status without actual cryptographic verification, bypassing the NodeSignatureVerifier step and causing validate() to count fraudulent signatures toward consensus, allowing gossip of a manipulated transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause validate() to enter an infinite loop or extremely long computation by supplying a collection with a very large number of unique fileHash keys in signatureHashMap, causing the outer for-loop over signatureHashMap.keySet() to iterate over millions of entries, consuming all CPU resources and preventing gossip of any transactions until the process is killed or times out?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the debugStake variable tracks only the maximum stake seen across all hash groups (not the stake of the consensus group), by injecting a high-stake signature with a unique fileHash that never reaches consensus, causing the error message to report a misleadingly high debugStake value that obscures the real reason consensus failed, hindering incident response and allowing repeated attacks to go undetected?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a collection of StreamFileSignature objects where all signatures have status VERIFIED but each has a node with getStake() == 0 and getTotalStake() == 0, causing getStakeRequiredForConsensus() to throw a SignatureVerificationException('Invalid total staking weight'), which prevents validate() from completing and blocks gossip of all transactions in the targeted stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() mutates the status of StreamFileSignature objects in-place (s.setStatus(CONSENSUS_REACHED)) without any synchronization, by triggering concurrent calls to validate() with overlapping signature collections, causing race conditions where some signatures are incorrectly marked as CONSENSUS_REACHED or have their status reset, leading to gossip of unverified transactions or suppression of valid ones?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a StreamFileSignature with status VERIFIED and a fileHash that is identical to the legitimate consensus group's fileHash but with a node that has getStake() == 0, causing the stake sum for the legitimate group to remain unchanged while the attacker's signature is counted in the group, and if the group reaches consensus, the attacker's zero-stake signature is also marked CONSENSUS_REACHED, potentially allowing a fraudulent node to participate in gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not check whether the collection contains signatures for the same node multiple times, by injecting N copies of a high-stake node's VERIFIED signature all with the same fileHash, causing the stake to be summed N times for that node, artificially reaching stakeRequiredForConsensus with a single colluding node, and allowing gossip of a transaction that only one node actually verified?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the consensusRatio bypass (BigDecimal.ZERO.equals(commonDownloaderProperties.getConsensusRatio())) is checked after the signatureHashMap is populated, meaning that if an attacker can ensure at least one VERIFIED signature exists in the map, the bypass triggers and returns without setting any signature to CONSENSUS_REACHED, causing downstream verifySignatures() to find no CONSENSUS_REACHED signatures and skip all nodes, preventing gossip of all transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a collection of StreamFileSignature objects where the first signature has a null filename (getFilename() returns null), causing the filename variable in validate() to be set to null, and when the SignatureVerificationException is thrown, the String.format() call with null filename produces a misleading error message, while also potentially causing a NullPointerException in downstream code that relies on the filename for retry logic, blocking gossip of the targeted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() uses a HashMultimap (which does not guarantee iteration order) for signatureHashMap, causing non-deterministic behavior in the outer for-loop over signatureHashMap.keySet(), where in some runs the legitimate consensus group is processed first and in others it is not, leading to intermittent consensus failures that are difficult to diagnose and that periodically block gossip of transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a StreamFileSignature with status VERIFIED and a fileHash whose hex representation is a very long string (e.g., from a 10MB byte array), causing the HashMultimap to use this string as a key, consuming excessive memory and CPU for string operations, leading to a denial-of-service that prevents gossip of all transactions in the current processing batch?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not validate the SignatureType of each StreamFileSignature, allowing an attacker to inject signatures with a null SignatureType that were incorrectly marked as VERIFIED by a buggy upstream component, causing these signatures to be counted toward consensus and allowing gossip of a transaction that was not properly cryptographically verified?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not check whether the StreamType of each signature matches the expected stream type for the current downloader, by injecting signatures from a different stream type (e.g., BALANCE instead of RECORD) that happen to have VERIFIED status, causing them to be counted toward consensus for the wrong stream file and potentially allowing gossip of an incorrect transaction set?
